stimulus: Sanjay Iyer, a plant geneticist at Cornell University, recently observed that ______blank share an unusual capacity for stress-induced flowering that allows them to seed under conditions that would normally suppress reproduction.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: brassicas, leafy crops that include kale, cabbage, and broccoli
brassicas—leafy crops—that include kale, cabbage, and broccoli,
brassicas—leafy crops that include kale, cabbage, and broccoli—
brassicas, leafy crops that include: kale, cabbage, and broccoli,

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is the punctuation of a supplementary element within a sentence. The dash after “brassicas” pairs with the dash after “broccoli” to clearly separate the supplementary element “leafy crops that include kale, cabbage, and broccoli” from the rest of the sentence. Because the supplementary element contains its own internal commas, paired dashes are needed at the outer boundaries.

Choice A is incorrect because commas can’t clearly set off a supplementary element that itself contains commas. Choice B is incorrect because the inserted inner dashes break the span apart and the trailing comma mismatches the opening dash. Choice D is incorrect because a colon and trailing comma fail to mark matched boundaries around the supplementary element.
